steps.description: 2.4 g of FeCl3·6H2O was mixed in 40 mL ethylene glycol and 20 mL ethyl alcohol solution.
1.06 g of urea and a specified amount of oleylamine (2 mL, 3 mL, or 4 mL for different sets) were added to the above solution and stirred until completely transparent.
The final solutions were transferred into 80 mL Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclaves and subjected to solvothermal process at 180°C for 15 hours.
The resultant black solutions were washed in alcohol several times.
The washed particles were dried at 60°C overnight.

synthesis.notes: Three different sets of particles were prepared by varying the amount of oleylamine: 2 mL, 3 mL, and 4 mL. With 2 mL oleylamine, particle size ~350 nm (shell ~80 nm); with 3 mL, ~280 nm (shell ~60 nm); with 4 mL, ~200 nm (shell ~35 nm). Ethylene glycol acts as a reducing agent to partially reduce Fe3+ to Fe2+. Urea decomposes to produce NH3 and CO2, which generates OH- ions to precipitate iron hydroxides. Prolonged heating converts the iron hydroxides to Fe3O4. Oleylamine forms micelles that serve as templates for hollow sphere formation.

steps.description: 2.4 gm of FeCl3, 6H2O was mixed in 40ml Ethylene Glycol (EG) and 20ml ethyl alcohol solution

Solutions were stirred until it became completely transparent

Particles were dried at 60 °C overnight
